highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.111: Opus 4.7 xhigh Effort Level & Auto Mode for Max Subscribers
highlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.111 introduces a new 'xhigh' effort level for Opus 4.7, sitting between 'high' and 'max' for a balance of speed and intelligence. You can now tune this via the /effort command, --effort flag, or model settings. Max subscribers also gain access to auto mode when using Opus 4.7.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113: Native Binary, Domain Blocking & More
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.113 switches the CLI from bundled JavaScript to a native Claude Code binary via platform-specific optional dependencies, which should improve performance and startup times. It also adds a sandbox.network.deniedDomains setting, letting you block specific domains even if a broader wildcard in allowedDomains would otherwise permit them — useful for tighter security in automated workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.110: Flicker-Free TUI, New /focus Command & Push Notifications
highlights.summary: The new /tui fullscreen command switches Claude Code to a flicker-free rendering mode within the same conversation, making long sessions much easier on the eyes. Ctrl+O now toggles verbose transcript only, while the new /focus command separately controls the focus view — giving you finer control over your terminal UI layout.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest flagship model with improved capabilities now available inside Claude Code. This release pairs directly with the new xhigh effort level and auto mode features landing in Claude Code v2.1.111, so updating your CLI unlocks the full Opus 4.7 experience.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code in just 30 minutes, making it a great starting point if you want a structured overview without committing to a multi-hour course. It covers the core workflow so you can get productive quickly inside your terminal or VS Code.

highlights.title: Security Concerns in Generative AI Coding Assistants (arXiv)
highlights.summary: A new academic paper on arXiv examines security risks introduced when using AI coding assistants like Claude Code, covering issues such as prompt injection, data leakage, and insecure code generation. Developers relying on AI tools in production should review this research to understand where human review and sandboxing remain critical.

highlights.title: Claude Code Routines Documentation
highlights.summary: Anthropic has published official documentation on Claude Code Routines, which allow you to define reusable, automated sequences of actions that Claude can execute on your behalf. This is a powerful feature for teams wanting to standardize repetitive coding workflows — worth bookmarking even if you're just getting started.

highlights.title: Master Claude Code in 12 Minutes (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This short Bilibili video walks complete beginners through Claude Code from zero to a working setup in just 12 minutes, with a focus on practical, hands-on steps. It's a concise Chinese-language alternative to longer tutorials if you prefer video walkthroughs.

tips_of_the_day: Use /effort xhigh in Claude Code when you need a task smarter than 'high' but faster than 'max' — it's the sweet spot for complex refactors without burning through your quota.

Lock down your Claude Code sandbox with sandbox.network.deniedDomains to block specific third-party services (e.g. telemetry endpoints) even when you use a broad wildcard in allowedDomains — great for corporate or sensitive environments.

Switch to /tui fullscreen before starting a long coding session to eliminate terminal flicker and keep your eyes fresh — you can return to normal mode at any time without losing your conversation context.
